description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the ubuntu:latest Docker image with two stages: test and deploy, and define variables BRANCH as $CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME, REPO as https://gitlab.com/johandurancerdas/gitlab-cicd-tutorial.git, and WORK_DIR as $CI_PROJECT_NAME. Add a test job in the test stage that runs a before_script installing openssh-client, creating ~/.ssh, starting ssh-agent, and writing a permissive SSH config; then in the script it runs ssh-add <(echo "$PRIVATE_KEY"), rm -rf .git, and ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ubuntu@"$TST_SERVER" "rm -rf ~/${WORK_DIR}; mkdir ~/${WORK_DIR}; git init; git clone -b ${BRANCH} ${REPO}; cd ${WORK_DIR}; npm install; npm install forever -g; npm run stop; npm run ci". The test job sets the environment name to Test with URL $TST_URL, runs on all branches except master, and has no artifacts or caches. Add a deploy job in the deploy stage with the same before_script, then runs ssh-add <(echo "$PRIVATE_KEY"), rm -rf .git, and ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ubuntu@"$PRD_SERVER" "rm -rf ~/${WORK_DIR}; mkdir ~/${WORK_DIR}; git init; git clone -b ${BRANCH} ${REPO}; cd ${WORK_DIR}; npm install; npm install forever -g; npm run stop; npm run start-background". The deploy job sets the environment name to Production with URL $PRD_URL, runs only on the master branch, and also has no artifacts or caches.
